configurationsTested: ad-hoc-signed CLI binary, Info.plist embedded via -sectcreate
ad-hoc-signed .app bundle with Info.plist + ad-hoc deep codesign
.app bundle with explicit reminders + calendars entitlements via codesign --entitlements
all of the above, detached from Conductor parent via nohup
conclusion: ad-hoc-signed binaries (CLI or .app) silently denied Reminders TCC access on macOS 26.4. Developer ID signing or App Store sandboxing required.
implications: M2 of icloud-mcp v3 design is NOT feasible as currently specified. Need to choose: (1) get developer cert + notarize, (2) rewrite M2 as a different distribution shape, (3) drop EventKit from v3 and accept VTODO-basic only.
